meaning: n.用肘轻推,轻推为引起注意
examples: Foreigners must use their power not simply to punish the country but to nudge it toward greater tolerance.
Nicky roused her with a gentle nudge.
He can work hard but he needs a nudge now and then.
This would involve launching a spacecraft able to give the asteroid a nudge to change its orbit.
The nudge, says Vemuri, helps users better remember the appointment or errand well before its appointed time.
